Title: Feature Request: Automated note creation
Body:
Wonderful application! I recently deployed it on the farm to record water dowsing reactions from dowsing rods for an area of over 400 hectares.

It would be wonderful to have an automated method of recording a note at the current position without needing to remove the device from my pocket.

On each survey I start recording a track log.

Every time there is a reaction I have to stop, remove the smartphone from my pocket, open its flip-cover, press the power button, tap the Note add icon, leave the default name "note", choose (the same) custom icon, tap the 'back' icon, close the flip-case, put the device back in my pocket.

With about 1/3 of the survey done I've recorded over 500 reactions (notes).

I export the project to KML each day and overlay it on Earth and try to identify the underground water-courses - primarily I'm in search of drainage and irrigation pipelines.

Being a programmer I can think of several ways to do this but this would be my preferred:

1. Add an "automation" feature that when enabled will, when one stands still for a user-defined period of time (e.g: 5 seconds), use a chosen template to create a note (or if implemented more generically, other templated objects) at the current location and then play a (user-chosen) sound to notify it has been done.
2. Create a note template that sets pre-defined text and icon. Potentially use a `printf` style to insert variable info into the note text (e.g: note index number).
3. Add an "automation configuration" tab where a template can be chosen (or defined) along with a stand-still duration. This would have an option "Do not trigger at same location unless X seconds has elapsed" and let user set X.

I might be able to tackle this but I primarily work on Linux kernel and core tooling in Debian.

Title: Stuck at spurious error in the welcome screen
Body:
* Install.
* Tap `Ok` on the warning dialog.
* Tap `Ok` for GPS access.
* Tap `Ok` for directory access.
* The GUI is stuck at the second step for storage permission: `An error occurred. Tap to view.` -> `Storage permission is mandatory to open SMASH.`
* Close and reopen the app.
* The app works perfectly.

SMASH 1.7.6 from F-droid on Samsung Android 7.1

Title: Looking for a way to survey on demand
Body:
I have a Skytraq bluetooth enabled RTK receiver.  Using Lefebvre Ntrip client, I can get an RTK signal I feed into the phone as a mock location.  I want to put the RTK antenna on a survey stick and activate waypoints manually every time I place the stick and hit a button.

I've gone through the manual but can't find any way to do this other than turning on the GPS track and setting it to a time delay, then make sure I have the stick placed before the next point fix.  Is there some way I can add a button to activate the fix manually into the GPS log, so I can then import that into something to precisely map a field?  This is for surveying farm fields for hydrological changes.

Title: navigate
Body:
It would be nice to be able to navigate to a vector point feature or to a note feature.

For example show a line on the map from current gps position with perhaps bearing and distance along it which gets updated as you travel towards the point.

Title: project export. How to retrieve data
Body:
In Geopaparazzi, when a project is exported to a KML file, the filled form's content appears in the file's structure, as shown in the image below (the orange part).
![Cattura_kml_geopap](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/101557593/223413701-077e6fee-617e-4571-944f-5b91be682725.JPG)

The data contained in the notes are not visible when the same procedure is done with the same KML file project exported with Smash, as shown in the image below.

Is that normal? There is a way to visualize data as in Geopaparazzi KML exported file? I need the data in the KML file because I use it to create a CSV table with the collected documentation. Are there other ways to obtain a CSV file from the project?

Also when I imported the GPKG file on a QGIS project, all the data are visualized in a single column, but I would need a separated column for each field of the form. I think I'm doing something wrong, is there anyone who could help me?

Title: Support for external receiver via NMEA over TCP
Body:
Some GNSS receivers could send a NMEA stream over TCP (port 1958 by default).
The receiver could be a client of your app (SMASH is the TCP server)…
or the server (SMASH is the client).

### Softwares
- [Vespucci](https://vespucci.io/help/en/GPS%20sources/) an OpenStreetMap editor

### GNSS receivers
- [Sparkfun RTK devices](https://github.com/sparkfun/SparkFun_RTK_Firmware/pull/309)
- [Emlid Reach M2](https://docs.emlid.com/reach/reachview-3/settings-overview/#position-streaming)

Title: Hosted Weblate Japanese language conflicts alert
Body:
Hosted Weblate alerts following Japanese language conflicts today.
https://hosted.weblate.org/projects/smash/smash-main-app/#alerts
> Weblate could not merge upstream changes while updating the repository.
> 
> ```
> Rebasing (1/1)
> error: could not apply 0e8fe69... Translated using Weblate (Japanese)
> Resolve all conflicts manually, mark them as resolved with
> "git add/rm <conflicted_files>", then run "git rebase --continue".
> You can instead skip this commit: run "git rebase --skip".
> To abort and get back to the state before "git rebase", run "git rebase --abort".
> Could not apply 0e8fe69... Translated using Weblate (Japanese)
> Auto-merging lib/l10n/intl_ja.arb
> CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in lib/l10n/intl_ja.arb
>  (1)
> ```
> 
> ## Typical workflow for fixing merge conflicts
> 
> 1. Commit all pending changes in Weblate and lock the translation component.
>     `wlc commit; wlc lock`
> 2. Add Weblate exported repository as a remote.
>     `git remote add weblate https://hosted.weblate.org/git/smash/smash-main-app/ ; git remote update weblate`
> 3. Merge Weblate changes and resolve any conflicts.
>     `git merge weblate/main`
> 4. Push changes into upstream repository.
>     `git push origin main`
> 5. Weblate should now be able to see updated repository and you can unlock it.
>     `wlc pull ; wlc unlock`
> 
> [Check the FAQ for info on how to resolve this.](https://docs.weblate.org/en/latest/faq.html#merge)

Title: Sharing exported data
Body:
Ciao,
the Export feature, what about an option to trigger the native "Share to" function? (the panel which asks the user  which app he wants to use).
Use case: I export data as gpx and send to my mailbox, or I save it on Telegram Saved Messages.

How it could be implemented:
- make the current list selectable through a radio button
- make another list with targets (save to phone, share)

( Now with new Android the saved files are in ///_Android_data/eu.hydrologis.smash/files/smash/export )

Title: Quick add workflow
Body:
This is the current workflow to add a custom note, starting from the map:
1. Press on the add button (document icon with the "+")
2. Click on the form
3. Open the section
4. Insert data
5. Press back
6. Repeat from 2 to 4 for each relevant section
7. Press back at the end to save
8. Repeat from 1 to 7 for each note of the same type that needs to be added 

This is the proposed workflow:
1. Press on the add button (document icon with the "+")
2. Insert data
3. Press next section
4. Repeat from 2 to 4 for section
5. Press "Add new"
6. Repeat from 2 to 5 for each node of the same type that needs to be added, or press on "save" for the last element

That kind of workflow would reduce the number of buttons to be pressed on the screen when many notes should be added sequentially.

Potential issue: should the same location be used for all the data?

Title: Custom SMASH apps
Body:
Hi,
it could be nice to give to the users a pre-configured SMASH app, without having to modify https://github.com/geopaparazzi/smashlibs/blob/0ea71496f5b33b5805b168b5213d342580b1c1fe/lib/com/hydrologis/flutterlibs/utils/preferences.dart. It is not trivial to found where the `shared_prefs` is saved.

It would be nice to share such settings and pre-pacakge SMASH with different defaults and extra options.

Here are some ideas:

- Different default settings, to ship an app with a GSS URL and password already defined or custom map theming
- Custom logo / name
- Single-project app (just open the default project and skip the first screen)
- Enable/disable functions:
  - Buttons: single note, form notes, track, layers, fences, ruler...
  - Menus: extras, ...
  - Settings: override device id...
  - First boot tour

SMASH has a config folder, which it is only used for debug messages. It would be nice to move the conf file there as first step.

What do you think about it?

Title: Download custom offline maps
Body: Smash can download a predefined set of offline maps, but not custom ones, while it can add custom online maps thanks to the import/export function for online sources. It would be nice to extend such capabilities for offline maps, so that a custom list with URLs of possible layers can be provided.

Title: Map should be the first visible in log profile view
Body: currently it is online OSM

Title: Add legend in profile view
Body: ... if maps are in paletted color

Title: When GPS is off map positions on Bolzano
Body: It should take the last known position.

Title: Inconsistent autocomplete behaviour
Body: Leaving it empty is OK (even if it is mandatory - could it be related to #130?), but if it is emptied, the last selection is kept. This also happens if the user types something incorrect.

Title: Mandatory text fields can be empty
Body: Is it by design?

Title: Avoid storing possible choices when adding data.
Body: For each point added to the map, Smash stores the whole form, including all the possible values and not just the one being selected. This can lead to an increased resource usage.

Title: Example tags.json from GeoPaparazzi is not fully supported by SMASH
Body:
As I am very interested in using the autocompleting string combo (useful for long lists), I copied `tags.json` from GeoPaparazzi example to SMASH. Even if SMASH says that it is able to load it correctly when starting up, many elements are missing.

- [ ] multiple choice combo
- [ ] combo that is connected to two others
- [x] autocomplete string combo
- [x] two connected autocomplete combos
- [x] sketches
- [ ] map

Title: GSS server: saving the password deletes the URL if not saved
Body:
Here is the correct workflow for setting up a GSS server:

1. Write the server URL
2. Click on the save/floppy disk icon on the right
3. Write the server password
4. Click on the save/floppy disk icon on the right

What could happen is the following:

1. Write the server URL
2. Write the server password
3. Click on the save/floppy disk icon on the right of the password
4. Server URL disappears

A global save button for all the fields available in the page could be slightly more user-friendly.

Title: [MANUAL] add clarification about geom type
Body: Currently *multi* geometry types are not supported. We need to add it to the editing section of the manual.

Title: Center the map view on GPS at startup
Body:
It would be helpful to have the map view centered on the current GPS position (if available) when the application starts. It the GPS position is not available then the last one is ok. 
The possibility to have last GPS position or current GPS position at startup could also be a setting and the default value set to current GPS position.

Title: Select to change attributes do not maintain the same zoom level for multiple selection
Body:
In case there are different features very close and I need to select one or more to change the attributes:
1. click in the center of all the group of features
2. one of the selected items will be displayed with the possibility to edit attributes
3. it is possible to browse the selected features to look for the desidered one
4. the second displayed feature have a different zoom respect from the original one and from the one of the previous feature, too small zoom that it is not easy to recognize the feature
5. if I browse all the features again all of them are correctly displayed with the same zoom.

Title: Download offline maps ends immediately
Body:
Trying to download an offline map, the process starts but suddently stops. The Completed attribute is shown in the maps list.
In the maps folder the main folder has been created (i.e. Europe) but no files are saved.

Title: Add a shapfile without the prj file
Body:
I tried to add a shapefile from the layers list, this shapefile didn't have the prj file.
The application starts to elaborate the data but the shapefile will be not displayed and not error messages are shown.
Maybe it would be useful to add two possibilities:
1. once the data has no prj star asking the projection to the user
2. display an error message requiring the missing information.

Title: Download offline maps
Body: I think that for slow internet connections the download map tool should work also when the device goes in stand by, or at least just pause and resume when the device is activated again.

Title: possible GPS filter
Body:
Per our discussion here: 

https://groups.google.com/d/msg/geopaparazzi-users/qj8bkUAohV0/GO3-SgonAgAJ

A possible enhancement (not a high priority), would be an option for position averaging. The behavior to the user would be:
1. somewhere in settings the user is able to the default number of positions averaged when collecting a stationary GPS point. Possibly an option to use/not use averaging as the default.
2. When the user collects a Note, the user is given the option to collect a 'quick note' or an 'averaged note'  (or, simply the default setting is used based on number 1, above). 
3. If the user chooses position averaging, then the user is told to keep the gadget steady at a single location. A notification bar begins a count. 
4. At any time, the user can stop averaging by pressing the notification bar. If so, all the points collected up to that time are averaged and this information is stored as the location for that Note. 
5. If the user does not manually stop the averaging, it continues until the default number of points are collected, then stops and stores the averaged information for that Note. The user is notified 'averaging complete'

Here are a few links to the theory and concept that averaging increases precision (but some folks argue: it does not necessarily increase accuracy). 

https://gis.stackexchange.com/questions/25798/how-much-sense-does-it-make-to-average-lat-lon-samples-in-order-to-increase-2d

http://gps-test-site.uga.edu/Files/MCFNS_9_1_14-21.pdf

https://github.com/davidvavra/GPS-Averaging

Title: Turn GPS off on iphone7 do not work
Body:
The Turn GPS off  command do not work in iphone7, it works correctly on iphone XS.
Simply it doesn't do anything, if you click the option in the menu it shows immediately the other option Turn GPS on, but after a second it comes back to Turn GPS off  and in fact the GPS continues to work.
This problems occured only when I overwrite the version of SMASH, if I delete the old version and reinstall the new one the button works.

```
{
    "compileOnSave": false,
    "compilerOptions": {
        "module": "ESNext",
        "skipLibCheck": true,
        "removeComments": true,
        "resolveJsonModule": true,

    },

}

```

Title: TheCocktailDB integration
Body:
https://www.thecocktaildb.com/

Another open source solution for cocktails that happens to have a lot more drinks listed.

Two ways this can go about:

-Add it all manually
-Integrate the Database into the App itself

Of course, I'm making this suggestion with the intent that you're at least partially aware of it. It would be interesting to see their clunky site in a more consumable fashion. There's thousands of attempts on GitHub too with apps and web clients, but none of them seem as easy or efficient to use as your app.

But that's just my suggestion. Depending on the answer, I can try making a list/sheet of all the missing Cocktails currently present on the database.

Title: Add option to hide values
Body:
Staring at a "bad" number might make one feel like they're failing at their goals and increase use.

There should be a way to "hide today's use" to prevent this guilt trigger while still keeping track of one's health

Title: Add option to remove outliers from statistics calculations
Body:
As a Daily user, I want to be able to exclude data points that are way too different from my average data point.

We can start with taking the 95 percentile and that will already exclude a lot of outliers.

- Went to a party and consumed more than usual
- Consumed using a different method which changes the price or quantity


- [ ] Checkbox to remember my option (which must be persistent)
- [ ] Take 95 percentile to calculate data on stats

Title: Widgets don't update after I leave the screen
Improving the UI Design of the app.
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Hi there, i feel this type of apps should have a strong influence over its users in order to make changes, and an intuitive & engaging UI can help this app to achieve it. I see there is a room of scope in improving the Design of the app.

Is this project open to accept UI design improvements so that i can contribute on them and code them as well

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I would like to improve the design of the app and code it as well, i am very good in UI Design and i've contributed to open source projects in design improvements before.


**Additional context**
Are the maintainers i mean this project is open to accept UI enhancements? please let me know, i can make some improvement mockups as well and share with you.

Title: 60 and 90 days line color in Stats page are too faint and confusable with the background color
Refactor SettingsView code
Body:
SettingsView is currently 658 lines long, and growing.
Most of the code is repetitive, and can probably be abstracted away.

This should be a priority before adding new configuration options.

Title: Add Detekt to pre-commit hook
Body: Waiting for the checks to run is a slow process. We should have detekt as part of the precommit hooks of this repository

Title: Add option to disable an enabled pause
Body:
"pausing a pause".

To prevent self guilt and worsening of a crisis, one should be able to say "hey Petals, I'm on a crisis right now, suspend the pause for a week"

Title: Add widget for use control to BangleJs
Add "buying date" and "buying amount+monetary value" option
Body:
I would want a way to add a secondary option for the "usage" button that adds a prompt of:

- amount bought,
- date,
- and currency value. 

**Problem solved:**
This way, I can track my consumption better and reduce it properly (ex. I smoke more than usual on the day of buying the stuff, so I can take it into better account if I can know when I bought the stuff. 

**Additional sub-feature** 
I would also like to know when I buy it, how much I buy, and for what price etc for average monthly/daily/yearly buying is possible, using the already existing graphs and data info 'screen'.) 


**Describe alternatives you've considered** none

**Additional context**
Really appreciate the work on the app, it really helped me reduce and track my usage, thank you :)

Title: Add K-Safe suggestion on pause screen
Body: Sometimes self-control can be hard. KSafes are good alternatives to take control out of oneself. That tip can be added to the pause feature

Title: Add option to purchase ad free option
Add rewarded ads options to "disable ads for a week"
Create a guide/gif/pdf on how to use the app displaying the main features and their purposes
Body:
In order to present Petals as a healthy choice and to indeed be a healthy choice, we need to show it to the world. One way of doing that is **showing screenshots**, which is static and not enough to visualize everything. Another way is to show to the person **face-to-face**, but that's not scalable and won't reach much people.

We need something designed to show how the app works, why the features are there and as an invite to get to know it better.
We should be able to show this 'brochure' to

- Potential users
- Researchers that want to understand the project
- Media/press

Title: Add code coverage metrics
Body:
Using kotlinx-kover https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx-kover we should try to estimate our current coverage by unit tests.

For example. I know that plenty of the database is covered in unit tests, but UI tests are lacking. How lacking? We don't know.

- [x] Add Kover to the project
- [ ] Create a github action to run it on every push

Title: Add automatic version bump to actions
Body:
Currently we have to manually bump the version with a commit before running a release.

That should be automatic

Title: Add automated detection for hard-coded string
Body: As a continuation to #119, we should have a setup to find if we have forgotten any unlocalized string in the code.

Title: Generate Fastlane screenshots automatically
Body: We should leverage our structure with Fastlane to take screenshots automatically on build/on demand

Title: Add break period to Stats graph
Body:
The stats page

<details>
<summary>Current Stats page</summary>

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1577251/195381217-5c32e920-e8df-440c-8cd3-5a932a28d2e8.png)

</details>


Can easily leverage the feature implemented in #86 , by using the PauseRepository

https://github.com/LeoColman/Petals/blob/29520622462f3229de86653f8c0136ddc46ef23b/app/src/main/kotlin/br/com/colman/petals/use/pause/repository/PauseRepository.kt#L16

to add the period to the stats graph, with a different saturation. For example:

<details>
<summary>Possible Stats page</summary>


![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1577251/195381805-ebcdf6dd-20a1-4b15-8950-2ed1bd4f826f.png)



</details>

Title: Add a way to track successful/failed pauses
Body:
With the Break/Pause feature, it would be interesting as an user to know how successful that pause period has been for me.

The app https://github.com/iSoron/uhabits displays habit-tracking in a beautiful way:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1577251/195380582-9400d328-aafb-4ce1-a889-45821daaaf52.png)


We can leverage that idea and include a tracker specifically for the break period, giving a bigger sense of accomplishment.


----

To add to the discussion, should the Pause feature get a page to itself?
If we plan to escalate the feature to include things like Tolerance breaks, a dialog is way too simple for this

Title: Add Auto-Export feature
Body:
As an user, I'd like an option to export data to a chosen file automatically


![Screenshot_20220924-152312_Petals](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1577251/192113146-2a096045-3acd-4a89-893f-d97d4e8f7ca0.png)

Title: Change date and time pickers to material design
Body:
We currently use https://github.com/vanpra/compose-material-dialogs
But Material Design is offering the same component, but with more customization 
https://material.io/components/time-pickers/android#using-time-pickers

Title: Add more information to use
Body:
One might want to classify their usage for different kinds of plants (for example, days of Sativa vs days of Indica) to analyze the data with more dimensions.

- [ ] Genetics (Bagweed, Sativa, Indica, ...)
- [ ] Consumption method (smoked, vaporized, bonged, ...)
- [ ] Objective/purpose ?
- [ ] Group Size/alone
- [ ] Possible triggers (anxiety, stress?)

Title: Add drug test information
Body: - [ ] Statistics of when it's still detectable

Title: Bump com.google.android.material:material from 1.10.0 to 1.11.0
Body:
Bumps [com.google.android.material:material](https://github.com/material-components/material-components-android) from 1.10.0 to 1.11.0.
Release notes

Sourced from com.google.android.material:material's releases.

1.11.0

Important

Elevation Overlays within default component styles have been replaced by the new Tonal Surface Color system. Instead of blending the Primary color with the Surface color based on an elevation value, components now use a specific Surface color role that can be more easily understood and customized.

Documentation for the affected components and which Surface color roles they use has been updated in https://github.com/material-components/material-components-android/commit/2114a11378fd801b32cd7431bb8a2c94de4f462c.

What's new since 1.10.0

  • New Tonal Surface Color system! Check out the design guidance and the commit with documentation updates for more details.
  • New Carousel variants! 1.11.0 introduces three new Carousel variants: center-aligned hero, uncontained, and fullscreen. Check out the Carousel documentation for more details.

Dependency Updates

None.

Note: Earlier versions of 1.11.0 used other versions of the androidx.activity 1.8.0 library dependency, but there is no change in the dependency from 1.10.0, the last stable MDC library version.

Library Updates

  • Badging
    • Adjusted badge vertical offset for certain components to fit design requirements (78cc54b8c39da437732ff3d7c96de8f345e09348)
    • Added note in attachBadgeDrawable method docs about menu item views being re-used when there is a structural change in the menu. Since badges are attached to the views, this may change the menu item that the badge is intended for (5e6ea582801ebfe2af8457261581a2ad43c8051a)
  • BottomNavigationView
    • Set badges to be null instead of removing them from the sparse array so that removing badges before restoring badge states will not override the current state (9f2e6864d2170d633709e7aa66b9e8e178352ef0)
  • BottomSheet
    • Fix sheet corners animation (de271320e4ba046c73e35e18f687ee4637f3a6ac)
  • Carousel
    • Ensure that extra small size is not larger than the large size (17baf71972016cf4608d9c83b902bb2debee585b)
    • Fixed crash when recyclerview has a size of zero. (26c31292012868d7e9889f6ecee7559c61caa5b2)
    • When navigating with keyboard, scroll focused item to nearest focal keyline, not the first focal keyline (fb9c1c6edfbdbe4791043a128c03a7fad5fd99f0)
    • Force hero strategy to be start-aligned if there are not enough items to make it center-aligned (9a2347bda5312dd6dc3ab7818a6f71297dcf018b)
    • Add logic for multibrowse strategy to change strategy when number of items is less than the number of keylines (cbb380df61f8eb66273043b437582c8058df7088)
    • Fixed focus order when using keyboard navigation for hero carousels. (0356f24a63b5c47ee23cbf5c0fca4d817e4d6acd)
    • Cleaning up multi-browse strategy and removing compact arrangement (ed4647d5dfa42876063521546de2dbcf71914e01)
    • Disallowing center aligned hero strategy with only 2 items since it does not make any sense. With only 2 items there can only be a start state and end state with the hero strategy. (d5d604d0ccfd73b4345dbea8fd3f833410fd2958)
    • Fix issue with uncontained carousel not having a proper end scroll value due to assumption made in end scroll calculation (8cb444b26856fdb8b0c894eccbf90133efc2a2b6)
    • Added tests for hero and multibrowse strategies when the carousel container is very small. (8312162f533d1c9fc9e9b15ceca58e3d1d4b61b5)
    • Update scroll offset to scroll to the estimated position that it was at upon an initial load (4a6ae4d0b6969311b41b3878e3c0e7f7a0824a72)
    • Fixed strategies crashing when there is not enough available space for a large and a small item (c418063205d74233a6235d7f7fd39be7deeb4bc7)
    • Tweak uncontained strategy logic to adjust medium size items to improve motion (93660d424139642320c85a098f0fb728126d4838)
    • Fixed formatted for KeylineState and KeylineStateList (b80d9a5ef4d93567554d5aac85c4070b4577d9fc)
    • Fixed keyline shifting in RTL for uncontained carousels (71517147110227e8032f5b064d6b59c5e3f117de)
    • Reduce the number of truncations in intermediate calculations (4ce7e4c8fa84ce05a6c34591855dd1207e44e42b)
    • Update vertical scroll speed to be faster (c6ea2d404023ca1a92cb95eb3f177579df1c2d28)
    • Fix some a11y bugs in Carousel (3d84841caddd6e0127e67721844a3900225ab3a9)
    • Center aligned uncontained carousel (b6f6eb555a0477322aa1ab4115f90d74d25a2266)
    • Add left-aligned uncontained strategy (9d81cac125144576501ca19dafa835625e5cf5c7)
    • Fix orientation not correct in item decoration calculations (966f7daec9d02092aadc63324212be9ae6dc8f2a)
    • Update mask size on size change if mask x percentage has been set (dc91b39d7c9af5a531a4f2f761297c6e44b8f08c)
    • Fix issue with next carousel item not being masked properly (a16f180ce7da65107305ba3a8c85db3e149767c1)
    • Add a layout listener to recyclerview to refresh keyline state upon size change (ff528621b3b550979c64f41aaec47438de30f061)
    • Remove default list from Carousel catalog demos as it is not an example of a carousel (0171624c16c799ca4e2dbe01e647a706fe7b895e)

... (truncated)

Title: Bump com.google.guava:guava from 32.1.3-jre to 33.0.0-jre
Body:
Bumps [com.google.guava:guava](https://github.com/google/guava) from 32.1.3-jre to 33.0.0-jre.
Release notes

Sourced from com.google.guava:guava's releases.

33.0.0

Maven

<dependency>
  <groupId>com.google.guava</groupId>
  <artifactId>guava</artifactId>
  <version>33.0.0-jre</version>
  <!-- or, for Android: -->
  <version>33.0.0-android</version>
</dependency>

Jar files

  • 33.0.0-jre.jar
  • 33.0.0-android.jar

Guava requires one runtime dependency, which you can download here:

  • failureaccess-1.0.1.jar

Javadoc

  • 33.0.0-jre
  • 33.0.0-android

JDiff

  • 33.0.0-jre vs. 32.1.3-jre
  • 33.0.0-android vs. 32.1.3-android
  • 33.0.0-android vs. 33.0.0-jre

Changelog

  • This version of guava-android contains some package-private methods whose signature includes the Java 8 Collector API. This is a test to identify any problems before we expose those methods publicly to users. Please report any problems that you encounter. (73dbf7ef26)
  • Changed various classes to catch Exception instead of RuntimeException even when only RuntimeException is theoretically possible. This can help code that throws undeclared exceptions, as some bytecode rewriters (e.g., Robolectric) and languages (e.g., Kotlin) do. (c294c23760, 747924e, b2baf48)
  • Added an Automatic-Module-Name to failureaccess, Guava's one strong runtime dependency. (280b5d2f60)
  • reflect: In guava-android only, removed Invokable.getAnnotatedReturnType() and Parameter.getAnnotatedType(). These methods never worked in an Android VM, and to reflect that, they were born @Deprecated, @Beta, and @DoNotCall. They're now preventing us from rolling out some new Android compatibility testing. This is the only binary-incompatible change in this release, and it should have no effect in practice. Still, we bump the major version number to follow Semantic Versioning. (045cd8428f)
  • util.concurrent: Changed our implementations to avoid eagerly initializing loggers during class loading. This can help performance, especially under Android. (4fe1df56bd)

Title: Allow managing dagschotels from within Hydra
Body: We have a list of products. We could indicate the "dagschotel", and allow changing/unsetting it. There is an API for this already.

Title: Paginate / load less Tab transactions
Body: We currently load all Tab transactions, which is not that useful and is not fast on older phones. If the API supports it, we could implement pagination.

Title: Test Tab/Tap integration
Body: See if we can test some of the functionality without requiring a Tab/Tap server.

Title: Move Hydra Play Store listing to new account
Body:
After some digging, I have found who is owner on the Play Store account used to publish Hydra. This owner is a personal Google Account, which is not what we want. A plan to fix this someday is:

1. Contact the owner to let him know of our plans.
2. Create a new developer account (either with the existing dsa@student.ugent.be account or a new one) (as DSA, where I conveniently work)
3. Request that the app be transferred to this new account.
4. Request that the owner close the existing account (and perhaps refund us the $25 registration fee, which would make this whole plan free)

Title: Button Animation not working properly
Body:
In Google Calculator you can tap on a button and it temporarily transforms into a squircle (or whatever that is).

In unitto, the animation is not properly implemented: The button has to be hold in order for the animation to work. The tranformation is imideatly interrupted when the finger leaves the display. This behaiviour should only occur when the Transformation is complete:

Current:
Tap -> Not enough time to trnasform
Hold -> Transforms and detransforms when finger is lifted

Expected:
Tap -> Complete transformation with imidieate detransformation AFTER the animation completed
Hold -> current

Title: Clearing or continuing with the result after tapping = should depend on whether the next input is a digit or an operator
Body:
Hi, thanks for your excellent app! There's one thing that's been bothering me since I started using it a few months ago, and I finally understand what it is.

After pressing `=` at the end of a calculation, most calculators will display the result and then:
- clear the result when a digit is pressed, starting a new calculation
- leave the result when an operator like `+` `-` `x` and `%` is pressed, and continue the calculation

All versions I've used up until Nadeshiko Pink _always_ left the result. Then Olive Green _always_ cleared the result (#117). Then Popstar introduced the setting _Clear input_ so the user can choose between these two behaviors (99a91be8ca67b10d891d7e2bdb2b9cba578eb057).

Wouldn't the most logical behavior be the one that most (all?) calculators use, as described above?

Title: Bump actions/setup-java from 2 to 4
Body:

Release notes

Sourced from actions/setup-java's releases.

v4.0.0

What's Changed

In the scope of this release, the version of the Node.js runtime was updated to 20. The majority of dependencies were updated to the latest versions. From now on, the code for the setup-java will run on Node.js 20 instead of Node.js 16.

Breaking changes

  • Update Node.js runtime to version 20 by @​aparnajyothi-y in actions/setup-java#558

Non-breaking changes

  • Adding support for microsoft openjdk 21.0.0 by @​ralfstuckert in actions/setup-java#546
  • Update @​actions/cache dependency and documentation by @​IvanZosimov in actions/setup-java#549
  • Implementation of the cache-dependency-path option to control caching dependency by @​itchyny in actions/setup-java#499

New Contributors

  • @​ralfstuckert made their first contribution in actions/setup-java#546
  • @​itchyny made their first contribution in actions/setup-java#499

Full Changelog: https://github.com/actions/setup-java/compare/v3...v4.0.0

v3.13.0

What's changed

In the scope of this release, support for Dragonwell JDK was added by @​Accelerator1996 in actions/setup-java#532

steps:
 - name: Checkout

 - name: Setup-java

  • Fix usage link within the README.md file by @​dassiorleando in actions/setup-java#525

New Contributors

Implement an preference for a command based control mode (left joystick replaced by 4 directional buttons : up, down, yaw right, yaw left) in combination with a takeoff command, kill command and land command.

Title: Enable firmware flashing over the air using BLE instead of just Crazyradio PA
Body: Currently, the Android client code has a feature to fail the firmware flash quickly when the USB Manager can't find a Crazyradio PA( in the BootloaderActivity). As the Android client connects to the Crazyflie directly over BLE as well, introducing a firmware flashing feature over BLE can be a good upgrade.

Title: Screen orientation should be locked while being connected

Title: Dark theme
Body: Add a dark theme that is easier on the eyes, especially at night.

Title: Improve information of firmware binary choice
Body:
The firmware update mechanism gets a list of the binaries from the latest release in our github release repository. A list of binaries is displayed to enable the user to pick the correct one for the update. Currently it is not possible to understand which binary to use and this should be improved, possibly by displaying the file name in the list or something similar.

An example is the 2007.05 release that adds two lines to the list saying
"CF1 & CF2 2017.05"

Title: No possible way of calibration
Body: When using the gyroscope on android device for flying the drone there doesn't seem to be a way to calibrate the gyroscope so that you don't have to hold the device flat.

Title: Implement bluetooth connection list
Body:
When using BLE there should be a way to select which Crazyflie 2.0 to connect to. Currently the Client will connect the first Crazyflie it sees (basically random) which is problematic if more than 1 Crazyflie is near-by.

It is currently not possible to change the Crazyflie Bluetooth name, but we can use the last 2 bytes of the mac address as differentiation. For example:
- Crazyflie 66C1
- Crazyflie 0A50
- ...

Title: [new-unit] Teaspoon
Body:
**Description**
The volume category is missing the teaspoon unit of measurement. This is a common measurement for cooking/baking. I suppose this is an accidental omission, considering that tablespoon, cup, and other food volume measurement units are present in the app. I don't know if this unit used to be in the app at some previous point, but teaspoon is omitted in the latest release, v4.2.0. I tested in the Android and web app versions.

According to the Wikipedia page (linked in the table below), there are two separate teaspoon units:
* metric teaspoon
* teaspoon (US)

**Units to be added**
| Unit name | Property | Reference to the conversion formula                |
| ----------| -------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| Metric Teaspoon  | Volume   | [Teaspoon on Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teaspoon)     |
| Teaspoon (US)  | Volume   | [Teaspoon on Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teaspoon)     |

Title: [new-unit] more currencies using other free exchange rate API
Body:
<!---
⚠️ Are asking about adding a new currency? ⚠️
Converter NOW is a free and open source project. The amount of donations it
receives are too low to pay an exchange rate api service. At the moment we are
using the free exchange rate api of the european central bank, it provides a lot
of conversions (but not all). You can check the supported currencies here:
https://www.ecb.europa.eu/stats/policy_and_exchange_rates/euro_reference_exchange_rates/html/index.en.html
Don't open a new issue if the currency you would like to see is not supported by
this service.
-->

**Description**
A bigger pool of currencies would be nice. Personally would love to have PYG available, I read the disclaimer on the issue template and understand that this app is not in a position to pay for a service, however there are free services available with more currencies than the european central bank, see https://open.er-api.com/v6/latest and it's docs: https://www.exchangerate-api.com/docs/free
It's rate limited but the app wouldn't need to access it that often.
I would still leave the european bank as fallback API since it's more likely to stay free for posterity, but it would be nice to use this to support less popular currencies.

Title: [BUG] Metric, US imperial, British imperial (Volume units)
Body:
**Describe the bug**
In "Volume", it's not clear which units refer to Metric, US imperial, or British imperial. This applies to gallons, quarts, pints, cups, tablespoons, and teaspoons. May also apply to other units.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to "Volume → Cups"
2. Click on "Cups"
3. Enter "1" Cups
4. 240ml = ???
5. 1 metric cup = 250ml, 1 US cup is 236.5882365ml, 1 British cup = 284.130625ml

**Expected behavior**
I'd like to have clearly labelled units for US, UK, and metric, relating to gallons, quarts, pints, cups, tablespoons, and teaspoons, and similar units of volume.

**Screenshots / gif**
N/A

**Device**
- Device type: Android
- OS version: Android 10
- Device type: Moto G Power

**Additional context**
N/A

Title: [BUG] "Tons" vs "Tonnes"
Body:
**Describe the bug**
Mass → Tons = metric _tonnes_, not imperial _tons_

(This is also a New unit request, is I'd like to see both metric tonnes and imperial tons)

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to Mass → Tons
2. Click on "Tons"
3. Enter "1"
4. Error: This converts to 1000kg, and 2204.622622 pounds

**Expected behavior**
"Tons" should refer to an imperial ton of 2000 pounds.
"Tonnes" should refer to a metric tonne of 1000kg

**Screenshots / gif**
N/A

**Device**
- Device type: Android
- OS version: Android 10
- Device type:  Moto G Power

**Additional context**
N/A

Title: [new-unit] Fractions to Decimal Representation
Body:
**Description**
Fractions to decimals and decimals to fractions is useful in many circumstances - Arithmetic, circle geometry and converting non-decimal measurement systems to metric (i.e. issue #148), etc. It is a standard on scientific calculators for this reason.


**Units to be added**
| Unit name | Property | Reference to the conversion formula                |
| ----------| -------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| Fractions  | Numeral System   | [link](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Decimal_representation#Conversion_to_fraction)                                                      |

Title: [new-conversion] pace <-> speed
Body:
**Description**
In the runner world, people often refers to pace (e.g. https://www.strava.com/running-pace-calculator) but for a lot of other cases, speed is used.
It would be handy to have a min/km <-> km/h (and a min/mi <-> mi/h for imperial units users) converter.

There are obviously websites which do the job (e.g. https://runbundle.com/tools/running-pace-speed-conversion-calculator) but I don't have to explain why an app can be better ! ;-)

Title: [Feature Request] Backup and restore config
Body: The ability to backup the whole app configuration to for eg. json file and later restore on a different device.

Title: Suggestion: add a metric showing how many kanjis in total have been reviewed at least once.
Body:
Whenever a kanji is practiced once, there's a green or yellow frame added to it, so there has to be a list of kanjis that have been practiced at least once.

It would be useful to know how many kanjis I have memorized or am in the process of memorizing.

Also, maybe statistics on how many times each kanji has been practiced.

Title: Rewritten alternative stroke evaluator
Body:
Sorry to keep you waiting for so long. Was quite tricky.

I've completely rewritten the alternate stroke evaluator. The new algorithm is a heavily modified version (not much left of the original) of the Waterman-Smith-Beyer-algorithm for global DNA sequence alignment (implemented something similar several years ago). I changed the distance matrix calculation and adapted the algorithm to work with two dimensional and continuous error values instead of discrete character data.

The algorithm aligns two sequences of stroke segments by keeping track of distances between all segments. If some segment of either path does not match a segment of the other path gaps are introduced. These still incur some error value but this way the matching of later segments will self-synchronize and errors will not pile up. Thus, the situation you described with character 級 is not present anymore (within reason). Too many gaps in a row will add more and more error (quadratic).

I also relaxed the error evaluation a bit to make the algorithm more forgiving. Difficulty can be set through a constant but I think it's quite good now. Maybe a bit too easy but you convinced me that this is the better approach.

Also deleted and reforked the project to appease GIT a bit. Pull request only changes two files.

Title: Writing tests should not show examples with similar kana
Body:
(Sorry if "kana" is the wrong name)

I noticed that when I was training hiragana, some examples were with kanas with diacritics, and vice versa. For example, example word for the "か" contains "が" (.. んがえ), it feels like cheating :)

Thanks for this awesome app!

Title: Icon
Body: Add material themed app icon

Title: Usage stats - enable/ disable within settings.
Body:
I like the idea of usage stats being sent to the developer, I agree it could be valuable source to improve the app.

Shame on me, but I don't remember if I allowed it or not, but I'd like those stats to be enabled. Title: Stroke recognition
Body:
Sometimes the stroke recognition drives me crazy. When you draw the kanji slowly enough there seems to be less of an issue but if you know some kanji really well and just want to get on with it and draw it more quickly, the app starts to fail to recognize some stroke up to a point where you'd "make" more than two mistakes and have to repeat the character. The whole thing seems to amplified by the fact that I use rugged devices with thick glass (low touch sensitivity). 
Anyway, lately I paid more attention to the recognition and tried to spot situations in which it would fail and I believe the algorithm mostly fails on perfectly horizontal or vertical strokes.
So I though I'd have a peek into the code and I found the following lines of code in DefaultKanjiStrokeEvaluator.kt:

```
        val widthScaleDiff = max(firstScale.width, secondScale.width) /
                min(firstScale.width, secondScale.width)
        val heightScaleDiff = max(firstScale.height, secondScale.height) /
                min(firstScale.height, secondScale.height)

        val relativeScaleError = 5f * (widthScaleDiff + heightScaleDiff)

        val firstScaledToSecond = centeredFirstPoints.scaled(
            scaleX = secondScale.width / firstScale.width,
            scaleY = secondScale.height / firstScale.height
        )
```

These seem problematic to me because `firstScale.width` might be `0`. I'm not sure I follow all the steps in the error estimation but a division by zero is sure to wreck your computation. I never programmed anything in Kotlin but the following could at least rectify the potential division by zero issue. I can't say whether it will improve stroke recognition, though:

```        
        val widthScaleDiff = max(firstScale.width, secondScale.width) /
                max(1.0, min(firstScale.width, secondScale.width))
        val heightScaleDiff = max(firstScale.height, secondScale.height) /
                max(1.0, min(firstScale.height, secondScale.height))

        val relativeScaleError = 5f * (widthScaleDiff + heightScaleDiff)

        val firstScaledToSecond = centeredFirstPoints.scaled(
            scaleX = secondScale.width / max(1.0, firstScale.width),
            scaleY = secondScale.height / max(1.0, firstScale.height)
        )
```

Title: Enabling "remove animations" setting on Android removes kanji stroke animations
Body:
The "remove animations" setting on Android removes the kanji stoke animations. The stroke animations should always be present, as they convey the stroke direction. Alternatively, the strokes should have an arrow pointing in the direction of the stroke.

I'm assuming that the setting can be overwritten, as I've seen other apps have animations, in spite of it.

Title: Tracking progress and learning quality.
Body:
I practice Kana and Kanji every day. Once I finish, I screenshot the summary screen and then compare the current results with the previous one, regarding both the overall time of the review and its accuracy level.

I think it could be great for Kanji Dojo to have built-in graphs representing those two factors - separate for each set of characters chosen by the user to practice. I think the optimal choice in terms of readability are two separate graphs: one presenting the review duration, and the other representing its accuracy level. Both graphs would show these data -for example - per month / per year (it could be great to be able to switch between the two).

An important thing: the graphs should be able to include multiple sessions if such happen - e.g., if a user has practiced a whole set of characters more than once during one day.


I think such a feature would be very useful both to be aware of the real quality of our learning / retention and our fluency growing over time.

What do You think? Do You like this idea? Would You be willing to give it a try?

Title: Option to offset daily reset time
Body:
Currently it looks like the review counters reset at midnight.  Occasionally it's just after that that I get my reviews in, which interferes with the daily limits.

It wouldbe useful to set a custom hour when the day resets -- e.g. Anki has such an option and defaults to 4am, which is probably a better default (as a time when more people are asleep).

Title: Backup / Restore.
Body: It would be of a great help and importance to be able to backup and restore the user's character sets in order to be able to smoothly continue learning and practice on (e.g.) another device or once the current device has been factory-reset.

Title: Apply SuperMemo algorithm.
Body: There is an algorithm of memorising out there which may make learning Kanji much more effective than traditional ways. Since I've seen its implementation within the other learning app (https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=pl.idedyk.android.japaneselearnhelper ; although it was about vocabulary, not drawing Kanji) I wonder would it be possible to implement this algorithm within the Kanji Dojo. The algorithm should be freely available to use (its older version should be open). It would take the learning effectiveness to a completely new level.

Title: Allow to choose user-preferred romanization system.
Body:
Recently romanization of some characters have been changed within the app, partially due to avoiding confusion between Kana characters pronounced/romanized the very same way. However, changing "ji" (じ) into "zi" may give much confusion, especially for English students, because many may find "ji" much more intuitive pronunciation-wise than "zi".

Therefore, given different romanization schools may fit various students, it may be very helpful to allow users to choose their own preferred romanization style within the app's settings (e.g., Hepburn, Nihon-shiki, Kunrei-shiki - see the implementation here: https://github.com/theblackwidower/KanaQuiz ) . Would You be willing to do so?

Title: Enhancement Request: Allow an option to sort the practices in the main page
Body:
When creating the practices, they are sorted in the order in which they were created. If we want to rearrange them, we'd need to delete them and recreate them in the order we want them.

An Edit option to move them to a different order would be more flexible, allowing us to move down practices we no longer need to review that often, bringing the new ones to the top.

Having just one button above the plus in the home screen to do my daily reviews would be immensely helpful particularly once i get to having 30+ groups of kanjis if i catch up with my wanikani.

Title: Reproducible builds
Body:
[Reproducible builds](https://f-droid.org/en/docs/Reproducible_Builds) means that the build process always produces a byte-per-byte identical APK. This is a security measure against a potential compromise at F-droid / Google.

The downside is that occasional bugs in the toolchain have historically made builds non-reproducible, so this is a long-term commitment for the maintainer. That said, F-droid is pushing for repro builds for new apps, whenever possible, so I think the toolchain should be fairly stable nowadays.

Related: #17

Title: Russian translation
Body:
I'm finishing up the translation the UI strings and the README file. I also replaced outdated links across all languages.

Things to mention:
- "changelog_contents" was not translated
- Names of people were transliterated (feels natural, but can hinder searching capabilities)
- Some non-translatable strings shouldn't be such, considering the Cyrillic alphabet and widely used equivalents. Strings in question are: "hiragana", "katakana", "kanji", "hiragana_title", "katakana_title", "kanji_title"
- Letter "ё" was not used (it is optional, but non-fluent Russian speakers might appreciate having it)

I also saw that you need these (you can shorten them by omitting "язык", it will also make them implicit and casual):
- App name: "Kakugo - Learning Japanese" -> "Kakugo - Изучаем японский язык"
- App description: "Learn Japanese with Kakugo" -> "Изучай японский язык с Kakugo"

Links: the [fork](https://github.com/Markov99/kakugo-ru) and the [compare page](https://github.com/blastrock/kakugo/compare/master...Markov99:kakugo-ru:master).

Title: Furigata or word+reading setting for vocabulary mode
Body:
I find the vocabulary section very awkward to try to use right now. Given, that's likely because I am trying to learn written Japanese prior to actually knowing spoken Japanese. However, I think there's a potential to make this work.

Essentially, if given a word like 口ごもる, I can't practically go for word->meaning without first going through word->reading. I find it awkward to have to pick going through either of them first, separate from the other. Ideally, I would be able to see and memorize all 3 - word, reading, and meaning - at once.

I think that could be accomplished quite easily by adding a setting to show Furigata on the Kanji in vocabulary mode; however, since I'm not sure if that data is available in an easily used format, an easier option might be to show both the word and the meaning at once. For instance instead of showing just "口ごもる", show "口ごもる (くちごもる)",

If this is something you would merge, I'm willing to try to implement it. I may do it anyway for my own use.

Title: JLPT vocabulary and kanji items
Body:
Hi,

In which extent does the kanji and vocabulary items refer to JLPT (Japanese Language Proficiency Test)? I couldn't find how it has been stated.

I first was surprised by the word 日ソ, which means Japan-Soviet, being in the JLPT N5 (real beginner, basic words - my case) list. 

But I started to really wonder today studiously learning "burasagaru" which is, accordingly to [Miageru website](https://miageru.net/vocab/%E3%81%B6%E3%82%89%E4%B8%8B%E3%81%8C%E3%82%8B)  , a rarely used verb of JLPT N1 (High level japanese speaker) level.

So, I thought that, maybe, the JLPT N5 80 items simple kanji list, which seems credible, was used to automatically create a 465 items vocabulary JLPT N5 list, ony made of the former one Kanjis (plus hiragana), regardless of the actual level of the final words.

As a conclusion, I think a clarification may be useful for final users to explain how those lists are made, or why they are difficult to determine or to create.

Title: Custom Reminder Notifications, Lighter Dark Theme, Dynamic Font Size & Button Size Adjustments
Body:
Would like to suggest the ability to add a custom study reminder notification. My suggestion would be: Time and Day Set an interval, for example once a day at xxx time or random time, once every 2 days, only on sundays and tuesdays, etc. What test would you like the notification to send you to? You would set a default test type, and amount of questions, and when you click the notification it would take you into a mini quiz. for example only 10 slides, then at the end it offers to keep going/into a full session or end daily session. I am also suggesting making a dark theme that's colors are more towards material dark grey colors, the dark black is tough on my eyes personally. On the phrases, the words wrap on the screen when in my opinion the font size should change dynamically. For example currently it would say "my dog is wh
ite" and break at the linebreak. As well as maybe adjusting some of the button sizes to be more accessible. Love the app however, appreciate your work! It's helping!

Title: Drawing lessons on complex writing are too sensitive
[FEATURE REQUEST] Vocabulary mode reading <-> meaning
Body:
I think it would be helpful to be able to learn vocabulary in modes Hiragana to Meaning and Meaning to Hiragana.

At least to me it feels easier and more natural to first learn English<->Hiragana then Hiragana<->Kanji instead of English<->Kanji then Kanji<->Hiragana. I find learning a Kanji by only knowing the meaning as an English word (but not the reading) quite hard.

Alternatively, a mode which shows all 3 of Meaning, Hiragana and Kanji would solve the issue also. To be precise, it would show Hiragana and Kanji together in the same field (one above the other, as child's books do).

Title: Self-verify mode
Body:
For the tests, could you implement a mode where instead of presenting the user with a list of possible answers, Kakugo just presents a single 'reveal' button and then lets the user self-verify if the answer he/she thought of was correct? 

I find that having just six choices is too big of a hint. I answer the quizzes perfectly, but I still confuse symbols when I find them in text where I don't have a list of choices.

Title: Kanji selection as a tree
Body:
the current selection interface makes it very hard to find a starting point to learn Kanji, with a highlight to the kanji composition lessons, where you need to have learned previous symbols to understand the ones selected, which is hard to find on the list.

would a tree of selection be easy to build? maybe dividing the current groups based on its level of complexity (i.e how deep the word mixing goes to a certain kanji) and have the program automatically select the relevant ones at the higher levels like a dependency management (with an option to disable, of course)? or perhaps a more complex and visual solution such as the trees of a skill tree that you would normally find in a videogame (i.e the most basic ones starting in a concentrated point at the center while the complex ones spread around and are connected by lines based on its connections in the writings)?

you and someone more can likely *expand* on this idea :p

what do you think? :3

###### sorry for taking so long to write this :( i should have written that sooner ;-;

Title: Drawing lessons to prevent bruteforcing

situation: you try to make the whole bar green before addingore letters to learn. then, you forget one, but still want the points for drawing correctly. the solution that you find is to try all the strokes you can think of until it makes the drawing.

maybe making it trigger a hint would solve the issue? like when many failed attempts are made, it should probably show a hint. maybe other subtle changes telling that it is okay to fail sometimes can be nice, too.

Title: Too jittery with background noise
Body:
I can't always be in perfectly silent environments when I need to tune. In real world use this app keeps jumping around even when I put Maximum Allowed Background Noise to 1% (see footnote 1). 

Proposed solution: Since the background noise is much quieter than the signal, a simple Minimum dB setting should solve the problem. 

By the way, another tuner app I tried (see footnote 2) had the same problem initially which makes me wonder if this issue is less noticable on other phones or operating system.  I'm using GrapheneOS on a Pixel 6 Pro.

____
Footnote 1:  The slider goes below 1%, but that just seems to make the app not function at all -- maybe it was supposed to handle fractional values in a logarithmic manner but is using integers?

____
Footnote 2:  On the other app, also called just "Tuner", I was able to workaround the issue by setting a Minimum Frequency. It would be nice if this app offered that as well, but only if it would not be too much trouble for the developers.

Title: Option to select the 7th Note in Solfege as "Ti" or "Si"
Body:
While translating this app into other language, I found the 7th note was called "Si" instead of "Ti". I suggest we can have a configuration to let users select to use "Si" or "Ti" since "Ti" is common to see and it's also okay if some users prefer to use "Si".

This is probably better to be a standalone option instead of bound to localization strings because even in the same region/country, a user can still have different preference to use either "Ti" or "Si". For example, in China, most people prefer using "Si" over "Ti", but there are still people prefer to use "Ti" since it won't be conflict with Solfege's chromatically altered syllables (in this case, [raised 5 will be Si](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solf%C3%A8ge#:~:text=/so%CA%8A/-,Raised%205,-Si)). 

See also: [Wikipedia: Solfege](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solf%C3%A8ge#:~:text=%22si%22%20was%20changed%20to%20%22ti%22)

See the previous discussions here: https://github.com/thetwom/Tuner/pull/57

Title: Feature request: option to display sharp/flat names side by side
Body:
Hi, inventor of ups/downs notation and the Kite guitar here. First of all, thank you for this wonderful app! The GUI is awesome! Seeing the tuning history in graph form is so nice!

An option to display the sharp name and the flat name side by side would be great. But in the lower half of the screen, this would make the tuning history window narrower. So perhaps the names would be one above the other? Or perhaps both names show up only in the top half of the screen?

Background: As a long-time microtonalist, I'm fine with the current state of the app. But we in the Kite guitar community do a lot of promoting 41edo to microtonal newbies. They have to go from 12edo straight to 41 without first using smaller edos like 17 or 22. Learning all the enharmonic equivalences can be overwhelming. The tuner they use is a natural place for them to learn that. True, they could use the #/b button to switch back and forth, but it's much easier for them if the 2 names are listed side by side.

Title: Feature request: custom temperaments (no need for GUI)
Body:
It would be great to support custom temperaments. It can be something not in the GUI, like the way https://billthefarmer.github.io/ctuner/ supports custom temperaments by the creation of a custom .txt file (incidentally, the most significant problem with that tuner itself for temperaments is that the software is limited to only 12 pitches per octave)

So, just a documented way for users to add their own temperaments would be big, even if they have to mess with text files and file directories rather than have a nice GUI for it.

P.S. calling these "temperaments" is technically imprecise because a tuning could be strictly Just Intonation, which is not a temperament, temperaments *temper* the pitches *away* from JI. So, "custom tuning" is the proper term, but I understand the confusion between instrument-tuning (like open strings) vs tuning as in pitch choices in a tuning system.

Title: A network request is not being handled on failure
Body:
Dear Developer!

My name is Alejandro Mazuera-Rozo, I am a PhD Student at Universidad de los Andes, and at Università della Svizzera italiana. I am part of a research on the usage of network libraries within Android apps. As result of this we identified some code locations that might have network related problems.

In this case, we address the code locations that are related to a network request which is not being assessed on failure. In order to avoid misbehaviour when performing network operations it is recommended to handle failure scenarios.

In order to address this issue we recommend:

1. To add a `try` statement allowing you to define a block of code to be assessed while being executed.
2. To include a `catch` statement in which one defines a block of code to be executed if an error occurs.

**Potential Code Location not handling failure scenarios when performing a network request**

https://github.com/wuan/bo-android/blob/2866251a4d2787d33f0841d36e1d0f4148a2b508/app/src/main/java/org/blitzortung/android/jsonrpc/HttpServiceClientDefault.kt#L39-L42

Title: alarm channels are confusing and make noise incorrectly
Body:
There are two kinds of alarms, labeled notification and signaling.  It seems obvious that signaling is more urgent, and there is a notion that notification is just a display and signaling makes noise.

However, i find that the notification alarm makes noise, and does so even when my phone is in priority mode.  And, I see only one notification channel (Lineage 15.1).

To resolve:
 - set up two notification channels, "distant" and "close"
 - default distant to "low", display and no sound
 - default close to "high", display/buzz/sound
 - ensure that both channels are not configured to override priority mode (by default)

Quiet hours (#44) is related, in that one might want to let close notifications override priority mode, but still suppress them at night.   Android really needs separate concepts of "user is asleep and wants noise only for emergencies" and "user does not want to have noise for non-important things".

Title: more frequent background queries only if lightning detected within set range

Once lightning is no longer detected within a set range queries could drop back to less frequent intervals of 60 or 90 minutes. This would allow for more timely alerts during lightning events but drop back to more battery friendly behavior when no lightning is within a set range. The 2 ranges could be based on the 'notification distance limit' and the 'signaling distance limit'.

Title: Can't use SDR# baseband recording with satdump?
Body: I have recorded a Meteor M2-3 LRPT signal with SDR#'s baseband recorder but, it saves it as a .wav format and I don't see an option in SatDump for WAV baseband recordings. Is there a way I can get the recorded signal to a format satdump can use? Or some other way to decode the LRPT signal.

Title: armadilo on Fedora 39
Body:
Recently Fedora updated armadilo from version 10 to version 12.

Now satdump-ui fails to launch as it seems to be hard coded to use libarmdilo.so.10

Adding a symbolic link 

ln -s /usr/lib64/libarmadilo.so.12 /usr/lib64/libarmadilo.so.10

fixes it ... for now.

Title: The actual frequency of the setting at LO Frequency offset
Body:
The signal receiving from the insat-3dr satellite in Windows 8.1.
I use a  in C-band LNB-converter with LO=5950 MHz.
The received frequency is 4781.5 MHz, in the converter it's subtracted from the LO frequency: 5950 - 4781.5 = 1169.5 MHz.

With unadjusted tuning scale, reception is successful at the frequency 1169.5 MHz.
![20240119_insat3dr_plutoplusip_noLO](https://github.com/SatDump/SatDump/assets/154206911/2525d809-bcf9-464d-857c-6fba583b9ae6)

If I set LO Frequency offset 5950 in the LO field, then the scale shows the actual reception frequency of 4781.5 MHz.
![20240119_insat3dr_plutoplusip_LO](https://github.com/SatDump/SatDump/assets/154206911/d975866f-f6af-430b-bdb7-e2d8f3ad7944)

However, there is no reception and I find a strange value in the log:
[07:11:21 - 19/01/2024] (T) Using PlutoSDR IP Address 192.168.64.61
[07:11:21 - 19/01/2024] (D) Set PlutoSDR samplerate to 8000000
[07:11:22 - 19/01/2024] (D) Set PlutoSDR frequency to 485532704
[07:11:22 - 19/01/2024] (D) Set PlutoSDR gain to 33, mode manual
[07:11:22 - 19/01/2024] (T) FFT Rate 66666, Samplerate 8000000, Final Size 65536, Skip 1130
[07:11:36 - 19/01/2024] (I) Waiting for the thread...
[07:11:36 - 19/01/2024] (I) Thread stopped
[07:11:36 - 19/01/2024] (I) Saving user config at ./config/settings.json

![20240119_insat3dr_log](https://github.com/SatDump/SatDump/assets/154206911/2a716cb4-2bda-4ee5-b824-3483f47c862e)

Title: Update command args for live goes_hrit in official docs
Body:
Hello, I noticed that on the official documentation page for the command-line usage it has an example for goes_hrit that is missing an 'e6' after the frequency value. Currently if you just copy and paste the example you get 0 dB SNR output. With the added e6 it should read:

`satdump live goes_hrit output_test_wtf --source rtlsdr --samplerate 2.4e6 --frequency 1694.1e6 --gain 40 --http_server 0.0.0.0:8080`

The page its on: https://www.satdump.org/posts/basic-usage/#live-processing

I wasn't sure where to report this, its so minor. I just had some trouble figuring it out when I blindly copied the command. Was hoping to save anyone else from the same trouble.

Title: Himawaricast decoder - compression error message Bzip2
Elektro L4 Misaligned Images with low SNR
Body:
I'm getting about 3-4 dB SNR on Elektro-L4, and images are coming in, but getting a perfect image is quite rare. Often the images are corrupted with misaligned sections.

This is using a 65cm discovery dish with linear feed. I realize the dish size might too small for Elektro, and also the feed is linear instead of circular, hence the SNR is probably too low resulting in some errors in the decoding. From what I've seen from others more than 6 dB is probably required for perfect images.

But is there any possible enhancements to the way SatDump stitches the segments together? For example in this image almost everything is there apart from some missing data in the lower right, and this appears to have caused a full misaligned segment. If the misaligned section was at least aligned the image would be usable.

![L4_8_20240112T093000Z](https://github.com/SatDump/SatDump/assets/17711170/e7f31008-4d13-40e2-8ace-fd0a4ce198e4)
![L4_4_20240112T123000Z](https://github.com/SatDump/SatDump/assets/17711170/d896153d-5da2-44da-a0b3-31eb4a343f47)

On the color images there is also often corruption with what looks like the color components not aligning.

![L4_221_20240112T063000Z](https://github.com/SatDump/SatDump/assets/17711170/902bc503-c138-4f3a-a3b3-abfde892ea89)

Also while I'm here, has anyone else noticed that while Elektro is circularly polarized, it also has a slight linear polarization to it? Optimizing the linear feed angle results in a difference of about 2 dB.

Title: Save record only when satellite is above selected minimum elevation
Body:
In Schedule and config menu there is presently an option about satellite minimum elevation.
This selects satellite passes with at least requested minimum elevation. Good.
However saved files on disk record the whole pass from AOS to LOS, not only when elevation is above minimum.
When using omnidirectional antenna such as a patch the signal SNR is not optimum due to low gain and satellite slant range. 
In order to reduce size of recorded files without interesting signal could it be possible to only save part of the pass over selected minimal elevation ?
Here is a complete record displayed with Audacity, showing the part with stronger signal.
![Capture d’écran du 2024-01-13 11-44-45](https://github.com/SatDump/SatDump/assets/14106669/edbfce35-f1e1-48cb-87cc-94e0957b8b28)

Title: Suggestion to add satellite name to recorded file name
Body:
I started to play with "Schedule and Config" option for automatic recording of different SARSAT 
satellite beacons. This is a very convenient process.

Presently the output name of saved files are of the form : 

2024-01-11_19-43-10_1024000SPS_1544500000Hz.wav

Although theses file names are already convenient to recall date, frequency and sample rate, this format lack satellite ID  that would help recovering the origin of signal recorded.

As different satellites telemetry may be captured I suggest to add satellite names in front, such as for example :  

NOAA-19-2024-01-11_19-43-10_1024000SPS_1544500000Hz.wav
or
METEOR-M2-3-2024-01-08_20-04-40_1024000SPS_1544500000Hz.wav

Consequently saved files objects would be a posteriori identified more easily.

What do you thinkabout it  ?

Title: Frequencies errors when setting manual Sample rate and LO offset
Body:
Let me explain my testing procedure.
I use an HF signal sent by a TS-790 transceiver tuned to 1296.100 MHz.
Satdump-ui is used for listening the test signal received by a RSPduo synchronized by GPS.

I selected 1296 MHz as receiving frequency and read the frequency under the mouse cursor.

Choosing any preselected samplerate gives correct absolute and relative frequencies reading.

If I enter a manual sample frequency equal or superior to 1.024 Msps absolute and relative frequencies reading are correct.

However entering manually a sample frequency under 900 Ksps gives wrong frequencies reading except for 250 Ksps.
For example choosing 480 Ksps lead to wrong frequency readings at 1296.19 MHz and 188.95 KHz. 

When introducing a LO offset things are getting bad.
First, when choosing pre selected 1.024 Msps, frequency is correctly displayed at 1.2961 GHz and offset 100 KHz is also correctly displayed when mouse cursor is over the peak on the spectrum.

However after introducing a 0.10000 LO offset the frequency is still correct but the peak offset at 1.2961 is now wrongly displayed 200 KHz, not 100 KHz.

The same for samplerates over 1024.
Strangely, with 250 Ksps samplerate entered manually the offset reading is back to a correct value of 100 KHz...

Selecting manual samplerate gives approximately the same results down to 960 Ksps frequency where offset is read 200 KHz.

These errors not only affects frequencies reading but also saved files frequencies.
This introduces unrecoverable issues when ones try to synchronize on telemetry beacon frequency.

Title: [Feature] Separate Num Avgs for FFT and Waterfall
Body:
Under Recorder > FFT, for usability sake I recommend having two different "Avg num", because the waterfall will show more signal features if the number of avgs is super low, like 1-5, but then that makes the PSD go nuts, I personally like to use 100 avgs for the FFT (at least when I'm at a high sample rate).  And by having a low default (e.g. 5) for the waterfall side, folks who are newer to RF who don't realize what they are missing out on will be taken care of =)

![image](https://github.com/SatDump/SatDump/assets/5722532/a25a7b4a-24fa-4cc9-9a6f-c9c59fcc3444)

Title: Automatic image rotation for POES sattelite images
Body:
**What would you want to be added to satdump?**
Hello Satdump Team, I think a automatic image rotation feature for images from polar earth orbiting satellites like the NOAAs or Meteors could be a useful addition.

**Describe the feature you'd like to have**
Functionality that automatically rotates the generated images based on the direction of the current overflight of the satellite of interest.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Manually rotating all the images if necessary.

**Additional context**
I think even a very simple algorithm could do this job pretty reliably: The algorithm calculates the Latitude of the satellite of interest at its AOS and at its LOS (this can be done with the celestrak-data). If the Latitude of the satellite has decreased during the time-span between AOS and LOS, its a north-to-south overflight, no rotation needed, but if the opposite is the case, the overflight went from south to north, thus the image has to be rotated.

Thanks 😄

Title: Example "autotrack_config.json" file
Body:
**What would you want to be added to satdump?**
Hello Satdump Team, some days ago I spotted the WIP "autotrack" option within the CLI version of satdump, but when I tried it out with

`satdump autotrack`

I just got

`(E) Usage : satdump autotrack autotrack_config.json`
`(E) Sample command :`
`(E) ./satdump autotrack autotrack_config.json`

so apparently the usage requires such an autotrack_config.json-file, but after looking through the project files, I failed to find such a file.

**Describe the feature you'd like to have**
Could you add an exemplary autotrack_config.json-file, or add an entry to the wiki explaining how to use the  commands necessary for such a config file, so we can write a custom autotrack config for the satellites we are interested in?

Thanks 😄

When exactly does make process perform copy in that directory ? make or make install does not seem to perform the update !

Title: Use integer numbers to display satdump-ui FFT Max, Min and Avg Num sliders
Body:
There is probably no need to display floating number values on FFT sliders for FFT Max, Min and Avg Num.
Using integer format to display those values would be perfect for dB schedules and average number of FFT.
To me it is rather strange to read that average number of FFT is actually 27.916 for example.
I would be quite satisfied by reading 27 !

Compliments for this fantastic application and continuous progress achieved by developers.

Bernard, 
f6bvp / ai7bg

Title: Issues with FY-4A LRIT and HRIT live decode
Body:
I rotated my 1.8m dish from GK-2A HRIT over to FY-4A last weekend as it'd been a while since I had checked it. I attempted to get both FY-4A LRIT as well as HRIT. SNR looked good for both services, but no data was decoded, encrypted or in the clear. Historically when I looked at it with my 1.2m offset I was able to get sync and data from both services. LRIT Demodulator looks very strange, and HRIT SNR values are over 2dB higher than my prior attempts that were successful. I attempted decimation of both 2 and 3, and yet I still couldn't get data out. I have a couple cadus from HRIT btw, sadly they weren't end to end as I started the record a little bit after the transmission began. Let me know if you'd like to see them. 

Satdump in use was from the past 2 weeks (I update pretty regularly). Tested on both Debian 12 and Ubuntu 22.04LTS.
